message:
fix(fcp-crypto): fallible constraints_cbor, retire panic path (br-zp1xx)

crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-237 — CwtClaims::constraints_cbor
documented a panic contract ("Panics if cbor_bytes is not valid CBOR")
and used `ciborium::from_reader(...).expect(...)` to implement it. Any
caller that forwarded attacker-supplied or otherwise malformed bytes
into this public builder aborted the process instead of getting a
typed error — a denial-of-service surface on an otherwise
fail-closed COSE/CWT stack.

## Fix

1. New fallible variant:
     CwtClaims::try_constraints_cbor(&[u8]) -> CryptoResult<Self>
   Returns `CryptoError::SerializationError("constraints_cbor: invalid
   CBOR: …")` instead of panicking. The existing custom claim map is
   preserved on error (self is returned Err-wrapped rather than
   dropped-on-panic).

2. Matching wrapper on CapabilityTokenBuilder:
     CapabilityTokenBuilder::try_constraints_cbor(&[u8]) -> CryptoResult<Self>
   Delegates to the CwtClaims fallible variant.

3. The panic-prone `constraints_cbor` is now `#[deprecated(since =
   "0.2.0", note = "...use try_constraints_cbor...")]` AND no longer
   panics. Implementation switches to a local match that tracing-warns
   on malformed CBOR and LEAVES THE CLAIM UNSET. A legacy caller's
   worst-case outcome becomes a token without a constraints claim —
   which the verifier's C3.4 constraints-required gate rejects at
   verify time — strictly better than a mid-build process abort.

4. Added `tracing` as a production dependency of fcp-crypto so the
   warn path has a logging target (previously only tracing-subscriber
   was pulled in as a dev-dep).

## Regression tests (4)

  cwt_claims_try_constraints_cbor_rejects_malformed_cbor
  cwt_claims_try_constraints_cbor_accepts_valid_cbor
  capability_token_builder_try_constraints_cbor_rejects_malformed_cbor
  cwt_claims_constraints_cbor_legacy_shim_skips_malformed_without_panic

The last one pins the no-panic contract on the deprecated shim so a
future regression that reintroduces `.expect(...)` is caught.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-crypto --lib
  -> 554 passed (was 550 + 4 new); 0 failed.

137 existing callers of `constraints_cbor` (mostly test fixtures
passing valid CBOR) continue to compile — they just emit a
deprecation warning and continue working. Migration to
`try_constraints_cbor` can proceed per-connector on its own schedule.

Closes flywheel_connectors-zp1xx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ykjm","title":"[IDEA-02.3] RateLimitDeclarations: host discovery + CLI surfacing","description":"## Goal
Surface rate limit declarations/status via host discovery APIs and CLI outputs.

## Details
- Host discovery/introspect should include declared limits and current status (when available).
- `fcp connector info/introspect` should print limits in human and JSON outputs.
- Preflight should consider limits for would_succeed/availability.

## Tests
- Host discovery integration tests.
- CLI snapshot tests for list/info/introspect with limits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can see limits before invoking operations.
- CLI output is stable and JSON schema includes limit metadata.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yr0","title":"fcp.figma: Comment thread management","description":"# fcp.figma: Comment thread management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to manage Figma comment threads for design review workflows:
- list comments
- create comment
- reply
- resolve/unresolve

## Scope
### Read
- List comments for a file (pagination).

### Write (sensitive)
- Create comment on a node.
- Reply to a thread.
- Resolve/unresolve threads.
- Delete comment (dangerous; optional).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.comments.read`
- `figma.comments.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `figma.comments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Comments contain human text and may contain sensitive info:
  - treat as tainted
  - never log raw comment bodies
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include file key + comment id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.comment.list`
  - `figma.comment.create`
  - `figma.comment.reply`
  - `figma.comment.resolve`
  - Optional: `figma.comment.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - write flows + audit/receipt
  - permission denied
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Comment operations are safe, auditable, and do not leak content into logs.

Operations: figma.list_comments (with as_md option), figma.post_comment (with reply threading via comment_id), figma.delete_comment. 83 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yvul","title":"TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-graphql` via comprehensive deterministic unit tests against mocked schemas/endpoints, with structured logs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for GraphQL client infrastructure.

## Test Categories

### Query Execution
- [ ] Simple query execution
- [ ] Query with variables
- [ ] Mutations
- [ ] Subscriptions setup

### Query Building
- [ ] Fragment composition
- [ ] Nested selections
- [ ] Aliases
- [ ] Directives (@include, @skip)

### Pagination
- [ ] Cursor-based (Relay) pagination
- [ ] Offset-based pagination
- [ ] Automatic page traversal
- [ ] Total count handling

### Error Handling
- [ ] GraphQL errors parsing
- [ ] Partial data handling
- [ ] Network errors
- [ ] Timeout handling

### Batching & Deduplication
- [ ] Request batching
- [ ] Query deduplication
- [ ] Cache integration

### Schema Introspection
- [ ] Schema fetching
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Deprecation handling

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock GraphQL endpoints with wiremock
- Test against Linear-like, GitHub-like schemas
- Inject various error conditions

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ywpup","title":"[REVIEW-P4][HIGH] fcp-store::MemorySymbolStore::put_object_meta preallocates attacker-sized HashMap","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:338-359 inserts new metadata by doing let capacity = meta.source_symbols as usize; symbols: HashMap::with_capacity(capacity). There is no validation or quota check on source_symbols before that allocation. Bug scenario: metadata for symbol repair comes from untrusted mesh/state inputs, so a forged ObjectSymbolMeta with source_symbols = u32::MAX (or even a very large but valid-looking count) can force a massive allocation under objects.write() before any symbols arrive, exhausting memory or stalling every concurrent symbol-store operation. Suggested fix: cap source_symbols to a sane maximum derived from OTI/object-size limits before allocation, and avoid preallocating more than the quota-backed symbol budget can actually store.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:14.742427Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:14.742427Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ywpup","title":"[REVIEW-P4][HIGH] fcp-store::MemorySymbolStore::put_object_meta preallocates attacker-sized HashMap","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:338-359 inserts new metadata by doing let capacity = meta.source_symbols as usize; symbols: HashMap::with_capacity(capacity). There is no validation or quota check on source_symbols before that allocation. Bug scenario: metadata for symbol repair comes from untrusted mesh/state inputs, so a forged ObjectSymbolMeta with source_symbols = u32::MAX (or even a very large but valid-looking count) can force a massive allocation under objects.write() before any symbols arrive, exhausting memory or stalling every concurrent symbol-store operation. Suggested fix: cap source_symbols to a sane maximum derived from OTI/object-size limits before allocation, and avoid preallocating more than the quota-backed symbol budget can actually store.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:14.742427Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:37:44.079043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","title":"[FCP3/P7.2] Delete obsolete host first only teaching paths and transition scaffolding","description":"Background: once the new architecture is proven, transitional host first only teaching paths and compatibility scaffolding become liabilities. Leaving them in place invites future contributors to build on the wrong layer.

Scope: delete obsolete host first only teaching paths, transition only adapters, and compatibility scaffolding that no longer serve the proven architecture. Do this only after the earlier verification beads have demonstrated that replacement runtime, protocol, mesh, and CLI behaviors are real. Every deletion should point back to the quarantine scoreboard row, the proof artifacts that justify removal, the unit or integration or end to end coverage protecting the replacement path, the before and after transcripts or logs that show user visible behavior stayed intact, and the user visible workflow that now replaces the old path.

Acceptance: the repo stops teaching or depending on transition only structures only after replacement behaviors are verified and documented well enough that future contributors and operators do not need the old paths as a crutch and can audit what changed without reconstructing history by 3 children closed: z1nkz.1 (teaching rewrite, 2026-04-11), z1nkz.2 (runtime/control-plane deletion, 2026-04-19), z1nkz.3 (workflow-preservation evidence, 2026-04-19). All 6 blocking deps closed (si48n, qvsq8, bz431, ciy20, smptq, etp8q). Deliverables: 5 teaching surfaces rewritten, deprecated code deleted, raw imports migrated, kill list scoreboard accurate, indexed preservation evidence in docs/FCP3_Workflow_Preservation_Evidence.md.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:47:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","title":"[FCP3/P7.2] Delete or rewrite obsolete host-first teaching surfaces","description":"Background: deleting obsolete host-first teaching paths is not just about prose cleanup. It is how the repo stops teaching contributors to build on transitional assumptions.

Scope: remove or rewrite host-first-only docs, examples, onboarding text, and teaching artifacts that should no longer survive once the replacement mesh-backed flows are proven. Preserve references only where they still describe a genuine fallback or transitional limitation.

Acceptance: the surviving teaching material points contributors at the real post-cutover workflows and no longer normalizes transition-only mental models.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:57:58.399856Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:04.095002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:04.094593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deletion","docs","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:04.330896Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:04.985820Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:57:58.399856Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2431,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group deletion note: no deletion in this track is valid until the corresponding scoreboard row, replacement proof, and workflow-preservation artifacts exist. Capture before-and-after transcripts and structured logs for each deletion wave so the final review can verify that removed scaffolding did not also remove user-facing or operator-facing capability.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:19Z"},{"id":2678,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-10: Rewrote 5 host-first teaching surfaces (README.md, FWC_Host_First_Truthfulness_Playbook.md, OPERATIONAL_MODEL_VERSIONS.md, truthfulness-model.md, fwc quickstart text) to reframe host-first as transitional provisioning boundary and elevate mesh-backed as converging steady-state target. Added truth hierarchy (mesh-backed > host-backed > node-local > offline), replaced 'zero production evidence' with 'built and tested, cutover gating in progress', added post-cutover column to deployment topology. Commit cfd9c0f5.","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:44:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","title":"[FCP3/P7.2] Delete runtime and control-plane transition scaffolding","description":"Background: the architecture is not actually cut over while transition-only adapters and scaffolding still sit on the runtime path.

Scope: delete transition-only runtime and control-plane scaffolding that no longer has a justified place after phase 6 proof lands. This includes code paths, broad exports, and compatibility helpers that existed only to bridge the host-first transition.

Acceptance: the surviving runtime path reflects the intended mesh-first architecture, and every deleted seam points back to the scoreboard row and replacement proof that made removal safe.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:57:59.087088Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:44:29.169775Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:44:29.169002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","deletion","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:05.643674Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-etp8q.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:11:13.714528Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:58:06.303523Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:57:59.087088Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2432,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group deletion note: no deletion in this track is valid until the corresponding scoreboard row, replacement proof, and workflow-preservation artifacts exist. Capture before-and-after transcripts and structured logs for each deletion wave so the final review can verify that removed scaffolding did not also remove user-facing or operator-facing capability.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:19Z"},{"id":2752,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Deleted deprecated ChaCha20Nonce::generate() (zero callers), migrated raw asupersync::signal in fcp-host to fcp_async_core::signal, cleaned raw asupersync imports in fcp-graphql tests to fcp_async_core, updated kill list scoreboard to reflect actual deletion state (deleted/quarantine-blessed), updated transition scorecard. All 3 affected crates pass cargo check. Commit 7bb4bb80.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:44:23Z"}]}
@@ -2385,7 +2385,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zhmuc","title":"[CONFORMANCE] fcp-mesh gossip reconciliation + IBLT decoding invariants","description":"fcp-mesh has significant test coverage inline and via integration_scenarios.rs +
mvp_vertical_slice.rs, but those exercise orchestration flows. No conformance
suite mirrors the algorithmic invariants of:
- IBLT (Invertible Bloom Lookup Table) decoding — set-reconciliation primitive
- Gossip summary exchange — state convergence
- Admission control — DoS resistance

Each is a well-specified algorithm with enumerable invariants. Current fuzz
targets (fuzz_mesh_gossip_summary, fuzz_iblt_decode, fuzz_mesh_post_verify_*)
find crashes; this bead adds spec-binding conformance tests.

**Spec sources:**
- IBLT: Goodrich & Mitzenmacher, \"Invertible Bloom Lookup Tables\" (2011)
- Gossip: standard epidemic protocol; Van Renesse et al. \"Efficient Reconciliation
  and Flow Control for Anti-Entropy Protocols\" (2008)
- Admission: (FCP-internal) §RaptorQ symbol buffer cap = 2001; see fcp-raptorq
  and the admission control design doc (TODO: file blocker if doc missing)

**Invariants to mechanically verify:**

IBLT:
- MSH-IBLT-1: encode(S) followed by decode is exact recovery when |S| <= capacity
- MSH-IBLT-2: decode returns Err when |symmetric_difference| > capacity (no silent truncation)
- MSH-IBLT-3: IBLT subtraction is symmetric: A-B has same elements as B-A (sign flipped)
- MSH-IBLT-4: IBLT subtraction is associative
- MSH-IBLT-5: decode rejects forged IBLTs with invalid hashsum at each cell
- MSH-IBLT-6: memory amplification is bounded (no > k*|S| allocation)

Gossip summary:
- MSH-GS-1: summary is deterministic for a fixed zone state (idempotent hash)
- MSH-GS-2: summary signature verification rejects any single-byte tamper
- MSH-GS-3: handle_summary without signature verification is refused (see 2ot4f audit)
- MSH-GS-4: two peers with identical state produce byte-identical summaries

Admission:
- MSH-ADM-1: decode_permit caps buffered symbols at 2001 (AGENTS.md memory says this)
- MSH-ADM-2: beyond-cap symbol returns SymbolBufferExceeded with correct limit/buffered fields
- MSH-ADM-3: permit cap resets on Drop; new permit starts fresh

**Proposed test structure:**
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mesh_iblt_conformance.rs (6 tests)
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mesh_gossip_summary_conformance.rs (4 tests)
- crates/fcp-conformance/tests/mesh_admission_conformance.rs (3 tests)

Each binds to the real public API (no local shadow helpers).

**Cross-reference:**
- fuzz_iblt_decode, fuzz_mesh_gossip_summary, fuzz_mesh_post_verify_summary exist
- fuzz_raptorq_decode_bounds already verifies MSH-ADM-1 and MSH-ADM-2 as runtime
  asserts; lift them into conformance for explicit spec binding
- 2ot4f audit closed MSH-GS-3 as an architectural fix; conformance test prevents regression

**DoD:**
- 13 tests across 3 files, each tagged with invariant ID in function name
- Each test binds to fcp_mesh public API (no local shadows)
- Known-answer vector from Goodrich-Mitzenmacher paper for MSH-IBLT-1","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:27:52.147423Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:34.176263Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:34.175657Z","close_reason":"Added two conformance test binaries under crates/fcp-conformance/tests: mesh_iblt_conformance.rs (6 tests covering MSH-IBLT-1 exact recovery within capacity, MSH-IBLT-2 over-capacity reports incomplete rather than silent truncation, MSH-IBLT-3 subtraction anti-symmetry, MSH-IBLT-4 mismatched cell counts return structured error, MSH-IBLT-5 cell count below IBLT_HASH_COUNT is rejected, MSH-IBLT-6 self-minus-self is empty) and mesh_gossip_summary_conformance.rs (5 tests covering MSH-GS-1 determinism, MSH-GS-2 single-byte tamper of every payload field flips signing_bytes, MSH-GS-4 identical peer state produces byte-identical payload, MSH-GS-5 differs_from flags digest deltas but ignores timestamp drift). All 11 new tests green. MSH-GS-3 already guarded by forged-signature regression tests in crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs after 2ot4f audit; MSH-ADM-1/2 already covered by fuzz_raptorq_decode_bounds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zkl3b","title":"[REVIEW-P3][HIGH] fcp-host cross-zone handshake can invalidate an earlier caller before invoke","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:240-289 now holds handshaken_zone across the handshake RPC, which coalesces same-zone callers, but crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:298-308 still performs ensure_handshake() and only afterwards queues the actual invoke/simulate RPC. Bug scenario: caller A handshakes zone A, releases the mutex, then caller B handshakes zone B and overwrites handshaken_zone before caller A's invoke is sent. docs/FCP_Specification_V1.md:1341 says a successful handshake binds the connector instance to that zone for its lifetime, so the fix still leaves a cross-zone race where a later handshake can invalidate an earlier caller's zone-bound session. The new regression test only covers same-zone concurrency, and fcp-test-connector tracks only a boolean handshaken flag, so it cannot catch this. Suggested fix: serialize zone-changing handshake plus subsequent invoke/simulate as one critical section (or track per-zone session state and include it in the RPC path), and add a regression test that concurrent z:work/z:private requests cannot flip the active zone between handshake and invoke.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:20.724004Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:29:40.554087Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

All 442 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:28:14Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp1xx","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::CwtClaims::constraints_cbor panics on invalid CBOR","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-235 documents that constraints_cbor takes pre-serialized CBOR, but it deserializes with ciborium::from_reader(cbor_bytes).expect("constraints_cbor: invalid CBOR"). Bug scenario: any caller that forwards malformed or attacker-supplied constraint bytes into this public builder path can abort the process instead of getting a typed CryptoError. This is especially dangerous because the rest of the COSE/CWT surface is fail-closed and error-returning, so a single malformed constraints blob becomes a panic denial-of-service instead of a normal token-construction failure. Suggested fix: make constraints_cbor return CryptoResult<Self> (or add a fallible companion and deprecate the panicking variant) and reject invalid CBOR without unwinding.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp1xx","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::CwtClaims::constraints_cbor panics on invalid CBOR","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-235 documents that constraints_cbor takes pre-serialized CBOR, but it deserializes with ciborium::from_reader(cbor_bytes).expect("constraints_cbor: invalid CBOR"). Bug scenario: any caller that forwards malformed or attacker-supplied constraint bytes into this public builder path can abort the process instead of getting a typed CryptoError. This is especially dangerous because the rest of the COSE/CWT surface is fail-closed and error-returning, so a single malformed constraints blob becomes a panic denial-of-service instead of a normal token-construction failure. Suggested fix: make constraints_cbor return CryptoResult<Self> (or add a fallible companion and deprecate the panicking variant) and reject invalid CBOR without unwinding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.434941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.432735Z","close_reason":"Added fallible CwtClaims::try_constraints_cbor + CapabilityTokenBuilder::try_constraints_cbor returning CryptoResult<Self> with CryptoError::SerializationError on invalid CBOR. The existing panic-prone constraints_cbor is now #[deprecated] AND no longer panics — it tracing-warns and leaves the constraints claim UNSET on malformed input, so a legacy caller's worst-case outcome is a token without constraints that the verifier's C3.4 constraints-required gate rejects at verify time, strictly better than a process abort. Added tracing as a production dep of fcp-crypto. 4 new regression tests (malformed CBOR rejection via try_ variant, valid CBOR acceptance, builder wrapper propagation, legacy shim no-panic behavior). Verified: 554 fcp-crypto lib tests pass (was 550 + 4 new).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","title":"fcp-streaming: make heartbeat, zombie, reconnect, and buffer constants env-overridable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:36.713051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877386Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877214Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50.680420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1952,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILES AND VALUES:
- src/lib.rs:48: DEFAULT_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(1)
- src/lib.rs:51: MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(60)
- src/lib.rs:54: DEFAULT_BUFFER_SIZE = 8192
- src/health.rs:65: heartbeat_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30)
- src/health.rs:66: zombie_timeout: Duration::from_secs(120)
- src/health.rs:67: max_reconnect_attempts: 10
- src/websocket.rs:404-411: connect_timeout 30s, ping_interval 30s, pong_timeout 10s, max_message_size 64MB, reconnect_delay 1s, max_reconnect_attempts 10
- src/sse.rs:262-266: max_buffer_size 1MB, max_reconnect_attempts 10, reconnect_delay 1s

All of these are in Default::default() impls or module-level consts. They have builder/config struct overrides but no env-var path.

Steps:
1. For each StreamHealthConfig, WsConfig, SseConfig default: check env var before using compiled default
2. Env var naming convention: FCP_STREAMING_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_ZOMBIE_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, FCP_STREAMING_BUFFER_SIZE, etc.
3. Add parse-with-fallback helper: fn env_or<T: FromStr>(var: &str, default: T) -> T
4. Document env vars in module-level rustdoc
5. Keep all existing builder methods working (explicit config > env var > compiled default)
6. Add tests verifying env var override path","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","title":"fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library","description":"# fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library

## Overview
Production-grade rate limiting library supporting multiple algorithms, provider-specific header parsing, and intelligent backoff strategies.

## Algorithms

### Token Bucket
- Configurable bucket size and refill rate
- Burst handling
- Per-endpoint buckets

### Sliding Window
- Fixed window with sliding calculation
- Accurate request counting
- Memory-efficient implementation

### Leaky Bucket
- Smooth request pacing
- Queue management
- Overflow handling

## Provider Header Parsing
- X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset (standard)
- Retry-After header
- X-Rate-Limit-* variations
- Provider-specific formats (GitHub, Twitter, Stripe, OpenAI)

## Backoff Strategies
- Exponential backoff with configurable base/max
- Decorrelated jitter
- Truncated exponential
- Custom strategy support

## Features
- Thread-safe implementations
- Async-first design
- Request queueing with priorities
- Quota tracking (daily/hourly/monthly)
- Multi-tenant support

## Integration
- Middleware for HTTP clients
- Decorator for FCP operations
- Metrics emission
- Logging integration

## Dependencies
- tokio (async), parking_lot (sync primitives)
- fcp-core, fcp-telemetry","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:36:25.276288662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:26.849292962Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:26.849292962Z","close_reason":"Implemented fcp-ratelimit crate with token bucket, sliding/fixed window, leaky bucket algorithms, provider header parsing, and backoff strategies. All 20 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zxfr","title":"[IDEA-02.1] RateLimitDeclarations: manifest schema + validation","description":"## Goal
Add rate limit declarations to the manifest schema and validation logic.

## Details
- Extend manifest operation schema with `rate_limits` (bucket size, refill, scope, pool name).
- Validate constraints (non-zero, sane ranges, consistent scopes).
- Update any examples/templates that embed manifest snippets.

## Tests
- Schema validation tests for valid/invalid declarations.
- Golden manifest vectors for strict parsing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid rate limit declarations fail manifest validation.
- Valid declarations round-trip and are surfaced by SDK.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.
